destination.name: Ukraine
destination.label: Ukraine — to the Defenders at the front

participants.name: Helen A.
participants.bio: A founding volunteer in Millbrook, where every postcard in the project passes through her hands before crossing the Atlantic.

participants.name: Helen B.
participants.bio: Hosts postcard-writing meetups in Montreal cafés, gathering postcrossers and newcomers around one table.

participants.name: Helen C.
participants.bio: Writes from Vancouver Island, tucking Pacific wildflowers and watercolour skies into every card.

participants.name: Helen D.
participants.bio: Discovered the project through a Ukrainian film screening in Los Angeles and has written every week since.

participants.name: Helen E.
participants.bio: Writes from Stuttgart, where her family once received letters across another war's front lines.

participants.name: Helen F.
participants.bio: From a small town on the Creuse river, she paints each card by hand before it begins its long journey east.

participants.name: Helen G.
participants.bio: Folds tropical sunshine into every card she sends from Singapore — the project's farthest-flung writing desk.

participants.name: Helen H.
participants.bio: Writes from Fukushima prefecture, where her town's own story of recovery shapes the hope she mails to Ukraine.

participants.name: Jean A.
participants.bio: Coordinates a Sydney writing circle that meets under the jacarandas every second Saturday.

participants.name: Jean B.
participants.bio: Sends cards bright with quetzals and rainforest green from Costa Rica's central valley.

participants.name: Jean C.
participants.bio: A retired postal worker in Miami who says she finally understands what the mail is really for.

participants.name: Jean D.
participants.bio: Writes with her grandchildren at the kitchen table in Kansas City — three generations, one envelope.

participants.name: Jean E.
participants.bio: From Europe's oldest port city, she mails cards that smell faintly of sea salt and orange blossom.

participants.name: Helen S.

media.title: Postcards — a beautiful way to support Defenders

participants.bio: Writes from Hobart, Tasmania. Her story of why postcards matter was published on the PTTF blog in July 2026.

participants.name: Debbie D.
participants.bio: An artist in Romsey, Victoria, whose hand-painted Christmas postcards became a story of art as healing.

participants.name: Oksana
participants.bio: Receives and distributes the cards in Ukraine, carrying thousands of messages the last mile to the Defenders.
